stories.title: Create End-to-End Integration Test for Full SAT Loop
stories.description: Build a comprehensive integration test that validates the complete SAT workflow: task file creation → daemon detection → orchestrator processing → PRD generation → response file creation. Test must be isolated (no production file impacts), fast (<60s), and cover edge cases like unicode, special characters, and concurrent files.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates task file in isolated directory with <User> marker
Daemon detects and processes task file
Status markers transition: <User> → <Working> → <Finished>
Orchestrator classifies task type correctly
Orchestrator generates valid PRD structure
Response files created with proper numbering (002_response.md, etc.)
Test runs in isolation (no impact on production files)
Test completes in under 60 seconds
Edge cases covered: special characters, unicode, long content, concurrent files

stories.title: Create ProgressBarGenerator Utility
stories.description: Build src/core/progress_bar.py to generate text-based progress bars for notifications. Utility should support customizable width, filled/empty characters, percentage display, and optional status text. Output format should be mobile-friendly and render correctly in ntfy notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: ProgressBarGenerator.generate(current, total) creates text progress bar
Default format: '[#####-----] 50% (5/10)' with configurable width
Supports custom filled/empty characters (e.g., '█' and '░')
Handles edge cases: 0/total, total/total, 1/large_number
Optional status text appended: '[###---] 50% - Installing dependencies'
Progress bar width adapts to terminal/notification constraints (default 10 chars)
Returns structured data: {bar: str, percentage: int, text: str}
Unit tests verify all formatting options and edge cases
Mobile-optimized: renders correctly in ntfy push notifications

stories.title: Add Ntfy Message ID Tracking to Orchestrator
stories.description: Extend src/orchestrator.py to track ntfy message IDs for update functionality. Implement message ID generation and storage. Update _send_notification() to support both creating new notifications and updating existing ones via X-ID header. Store message IDs in task metadata for persistence across process restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: _send_notification() accepts optional message_id parameter
When message_id provided, adds 'X-ID' header for message replacement
Message IDs generated as unique strings (format: 'sat-{task_id}-progress')
Message ID stored in task metadata (.sat/notification_ids/{task_id}.json)
Supports creating new notification (no message_id) and updating existing (with message_id)
Graceful handling: if ntfy update fails, falls back to new notification
Unit tests verify message ID tracking and update logic
Integration test verifies ntfy receives correct X-ID header

stories.title: Implement Progress Tracking in Orchestrator
stories.description: Add story-level progress tracking to orchestrator.py. Track total stories, completed stories, and current story for each task. Expose progress data to notification system. Update progress after each story phase completion. Ensure progress persists to Ralph Pro progress.json for consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator tracks: total_stories, completed_stories, current_story, current_phase
Progress updates after each story completion
Progress data synchronized with Ralph Pro progress.json
get_progress() method returns: {total: int, completed: int, current: str, percentage: int}
Progress includes phase-level granularity (e.g., 'US-003: TDD-GREEN')
Progress calculation handles edge cases: 0 stories, all stories complete
Unit tests verify progress tracking across story lifecycle
Integration test verifies progress sync with Ralph Pro

stories.title: Update Orchestrator Notifications to Use Progress Bar
stories.description: Refactor orchestrator.py notification calls to use single updating notification with progress bar. Replace multiple story notifications with one notification that updates on each story completion. Include progress bar, story count, and current story name in notification message.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator sends initial notification when task starts with 0% progress
Notification updates after each story completion with updated progress bar
Final notification at 100% includes completion status and duration
Failed stories update notification with error indicator
Uses consistent message_id for all updates within a task
Unit tests verify notification updates at each story transition
Integration test verifies single notification thread in ntfy

stories.title: Add Progress Bar Support to Ralph Pro Integration
stories.description: Extend Ralph Pro integration in orchestrator.py to monitor progress during Ralph Pro execution. Parse Ralph Pro output to detect story transitions and update progress notification in real-time. Handle Ralph Pro's async execution while maintaining notification updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator monitors Ralph Pro stdout for story transition events
Parses Ralph Pro progress updates (e.g., 'Starting story US-003', 'Completed story US-002')
Updates progress notification when story transitions detected
Non-blocking: monitoring doesn't block Ralph Pro execution
Handles Ralph Pro output buffering (flushes/line buffering)
Graceful degradation: if parsing fails, falls back to periodic progress checks
Unit tests verify output parsing and progress detection
Integration test verifies real-time notification updates during Ralph Pro execution

stories.title: Add Progress Bar to Daemon Task Processing
stories.description: Integrate progress bar notifications into src/daemon.py for task lifecycle tracking. Daemon should send progress updates as tasks transition through states: detected → decomposing → executing → complete. Ensure daemon notifications use same message ID pattern for consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Daemon sends initial notification when task file detected
Daemon updates notification when task decomposition starts
Daemon updates notification when Ralph Pro execution begins
Daemon delegates to orchestrator for story-level progress updates
Daemon sends final notification with task completion status
All daemon notifications use consistent message_id (format: 'sat-daemon-{task_name}')
Progress updates include high-level task lifecycle stages
Unit tests verify daemon notification flow
Integration test verifies end-to-end: file detected → decomposed → executed → completed with single notification thread

stories.title: Add Progress Bar Configuration and Documentation
stories.description: Document ntfy progress bar feature in CLAUDE.md including message update mechanism, progress bar format, and notification flow. Add configuration options for progress bar appearance (.sat/notification_config.json). Create examples showing notification progression. Include troubleshooting guide for notification update failures.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md includes Ntfy Progress Notifications section with architecture diagram
Message update mechanism documented: X-ID header usage, message ID format
Progress bar format documented with customization options
Notification flow documented: task start → story updates → completion
Configuration schema documented: progress_bar_width, filled_char, empty_char, update_frequency
Example notification progression screenshots/text samples
Troubleshooting section: ntfy unreachable, update failures, message ID conflicts
README updated with progress notification feature overview
Example notification_config.json with defaults

stories.title: Set up Amazon SES SMTP credentials and verify domain
stories.description: Configure Amazon SES in the appropriate AWS region (us-east-1). Create SMTP credentials for Mautic to use. Verify the sending domain (logicalfront.com) in SES. This story focuses on the AWS SES setup and initial domain verification process.
acceptanceCriteria: SES SMTP credentials are created and securely stored
Domain logicalfront.com is verified in SES
SES is moved out of sandbox mode (or sandbox limits documented)
Configuration details are documented for Mautic integration

stories.title: Generate and document required DNS records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC)
stories.description: Generate all DNS records required for email authentication and deliverability. This includes SPF records authorizing SES to send for logicalfront.com, DKIM records for email signing, and DMARC policy records. Document the exact TXT records that need to be added to DNS.
acceptanceCriteria: SPF TXT record is generated and documented
DKIM CNAME records are generated and documented
DMARC TXT record is generated with appropriate policy (p=none for initial setup)
All DNS records are formatted correctly and ready to apply
Documentation includes record names, types, and values

stories.title: Configure SES bounce and complaint handling
stories.description: Set up SNS topics and subscriptions to handle bounce and complaint notifications from SES. Configure SES to send notifications to these SNS topics. This ensures email reputation is maintained and Mautic can process bounces appropriately.
acceptanceCriteria: SNS topics created for bounces and complaints
SES configured to publish bounce/complaint events to SNS
Webhook or email endpoint configured to receive SNS notifications
Documentation includes SNS topic ARNs and subscription details

stories.title: Configure Mautic email transport with SES SMTP credentials
stories.description: Access the Mautic Docker container at port 8080 (marketing.logicalfront.com) and configure the email settings. Set the transport to 'Amazon SES' or 'Other SMTP' and input the SMTP credentials from US-053. Test the connection within Mautic's configuration interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Mautic email configuration is set to use SES SMTP
SMTP host, port, username, and password are correctly configured
From address is set to a verified email at logicalfront.com
Mautic connection test to SES succeeds
Configuration is persisted in Mautic container

stories.title: Configure Mautic sending limits for email warmup
stories.description: Set up email sending limits in Mautic to match the warmup schedule. Configure Mautic to start with 50 emails/day and document the 4-week ramp-up schedule. This prevents reputation damage from sending too many emails on a new domain.
acceptanceCriteria: Mautic email frequency rules configured for 50 emails/day initially
4-week warmup schedule documented with daily/weekly sending limits
Schedule includes: Week 1 (50/day), Week 2 (100/day), Week 3 (250/day), Week 4 (500/day)
Documentation includes how to adjust limits in Mautic as warmup progresses
Monitoring plan documented to track sending volume and reputation

stories.title: Create test script to verify email delivery pipeline
stories.description: Build a TDD-based verification script that sends a test email through Mautic via SES, verifies SPF/DKIM alignment using email headers, and confirms bounce handling works. The script should be reusable for ongoing monitoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Test script sends email via Mautic API or SMTP
Script retrieves and parses email headers to verify SPF/DKIM pass
Script tests bounce handling by sending to known bounce address
Script outputs clear pass/fail status for each verification
Script is documented with usage instructions
All tests pass when DNS records are applied and pipeline is configured

stories.title: Document complete setup procedure and ongoing maintenance
stories.description: Create comprehensive documentation covering the entire SES+Mautic setup, DNS record application, warmup schedule execution, and ongoing monitoring procedures. Include troubleshooting steps and common issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation includes step-by-step setup procedure
DNS records are listed in copy-paste ready format
Warmup schedule with calendar dates and sending limits
Monitoring procedures for bounce rates and reputation
Troubleshooting guide for common SES and Mautic issues
Maintenance tasks documented (credential rotation, monitoring reviews)

stories.title: Configure N8N API credentials for enrichment services
stories.description: Set up N8N credential manager at port 8090 (automate.logicalfront.com) with API keys for all external enrichment services: Apollo.io (company/firmographic data), RB2B (website visitor identification), IPinfo (IP-to-company resolution), and Apify (web scraping). Verify connectivity and test API calls for each service.
acceptanceCriteria: Apollo.io API credentials added to N8N and connection tested
RB2B webhook URL configured and test webhook received
IPinfo API credentials added to N8N and test query succeeds
Apify API credentials added to N8N and test actor run completes
All credentials stored securely in N8N credential manager
Test API calls documented with sample responses

stories.title: Build Lead Enrichment Workflow with Apollo integration
stories.description: Create N8N workflow triggered by Mautic contact creation webhook. Extract email domain from new contact, query Apollo.io API for company firmographics (size, industry, revenue, employee count), parse API response, and update Mautic contact with enriched fields. Include error handling for API failures and rate limits.
acceptanceCriteria: N8N workflow listens for Mautic 'contact.created' webhook
Workflow extracts email domain from contact data
Apollo.io API called with domain to fetch company data
Company size, industry, revenue, employee count parsed from response
Mautic contact updated via API with enriched fields
Error handling: API timeout, rate limit, no data found scenarios
Test script creates Mautic contact and verifies enrichment occurs
All tests pass with mock Apollo API responses

stories.title: Build Website Visitor Identification Workflow with RB2B
stories.description: Create N8N workflow that receives RB2B webhook data when a website visitor is identified. Match visitor email/company to existing Mautic contacts. Create new lead in Mautic if no match found. Route high-value visitors (based on company size/industry filters) to SuiteCRM as opportunities. Include visitor activity timeline logging.
acceptanceCriteria: N8N workflow receives RB2B webhook with visitor identification data
Workflow searches Mautic contacts by email and company name
If match found, append visitor activity to contact timeline
If no match, create new lead in Mautic with RB2B source tag
High-value visitors (company size >100 employees) routed to SuiteCRM
SuiteCRM opportunity created with visitor context and page views
Test script sends mock RB2B webhook and verifies routing logic
All tests pass with various visitor scenarios

stories.title: Build IP Intelligence Workflow with IPinfo integration
stories.description: Create N8N workflow to enrich anonymous website visitors using IPinfo API for IP-to-company resolution. Accept visitor IP address from analytics/tracking system, query IPinfo for company data, create preliminary Mautic contact with company context, and trigger Apollo enrichment workflow for identified companies.
acceptanceCriteria: N8N workflow accepts visitor IP address via webhook or manual trigger
IPinfo API called with IP to resolve company name and location
Company name, industry, and employee count extracted from IPinfo
Preliminary Mautic contact created with company context (no email yet)
Workflow chains to Apollo enrichment using company domain
Error handling: private IPs, VPNs, residential IPs (no company data)
Test script submits known company IP and verifies enrichment chain
All tests pass with mock IPinfo responses

stories.title: Build Web Scraping Pipeline with Apify integration
stories.description: Create N8N workflow that uses Apify actors to scrape publicly available contact information from target organizations. Accept target URLs (LinkedIn company pages, corporate websites), configure Apify LinkedIn/website scrapers, parse scraped data for contacts (name, title, email), create Mautic leads with 'Apify' source tag, and apply lead scoring based on title/seniority.
acceptanceCriteria: N8N workflow accepts target URLs (LinkedIn or company website)
Apify LinkedIn scraper actor triggered with company URL
Apify website scraper actor triggered for contact pages
Scraped contact data parsed (name, email, title, company)
Mautic leads created with 'Apify' source tag and original URL
Lead scoring applied: C-level (50 points), VP (40), Director (30), Manager (20)
Duplicate detection: skip if email already exists in Mautic
Test script triggers scraping workflow and verifies lead creation
All tests pass with mock Apify actor responses

stories.title: Implement error handling and rate limit management for all workflows
stories.description: Add comprehensive error handling to all N8N enrichment workflows (US-061 through US-064). Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for API failures, rate limit detection and queuing, dead-letter queue for failed enrichments, and monitoring/alerting via ntfy notifications for critical workflow failures.
acceptanceCriteria: All workflows have try-catch error nodes for API calls
API rate limit errors (429) trigger exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s)
Maximum 3 retry attempts before moving to dead-letter queue
Failed enrichments logged to N8N workflow error table with context
Critical failures send ntfy notification to johnlane-claude-tasks
Rate limit queue implemented: pause workflow when quota reached
Test script triggers rate limit scenario and verifies backoff logic
All error handling tests pass with mock API failures

stories.title: Create end-to-end verification tests for all enrichment workflows
stories.description: Build comprehensive test suite to verify all N8N enrichment workflows with sample data. Test Apollo enrichment, RB2B visitor identification, IPinfo IP resolution, Apify web scraping, error handling, and data population in Mautic/SuiteCRM. Verify enrichment data quality and workflow reliability.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates sample Mautic contact and verifies Apollo enrichment populates fields
Test sends mock RB2B webhook and verifies contact creation/update in Mautic
Test verifies high-value RB2B visitors create SuiteCRM opportunities
Test submits IP address and verifies IPinfo company resolution and enrichment
Test triggers Apify workflow and verifies scraped leads appear in Mautic
Test verifies rate limit handling doesn't break workflows
Test verifies duplicate detection prevents duplicate contacts
All tests pass with >90% workflow node coverage
Test cleanup script removes test data from Mautic/SuiteCRM

stories.title: Document N8N enrichment workflows and operational procedures
stories.description: Create comprehensive documentation for all N8N data enrichment workflows. Include architecture diagrams, API credential setup, workflow configuration, field mappings, monitoring procedures, troubleshooting guide, rate limit management, and data quality assessment. Document workflow dependencies and execution order.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture diagram shows data flow: N8N ↔ Mautic ↔ SuiteCRM ↔ External APIs
API credential setup guide for Apollo, RB2B, IPinfo, Apify
Each workflow documented: trigger, nodes, logic, output fields
Field mapping tables: Apollo → Mautic, RB2B → Mautic/SuiteCRM, etc.
Monitoring procedures: workflow execution logs, API quota tracking, error rates
Troubleshooting guide: common API errors, webhook failures, data quality issues
Rate limit management: quotas per service, queuing strategy, backoff timings
Workflow dependency diagram showing execution order and chaining

stories.title: Configure Ollama Local LLM Models for Marketing Tasks
stories.description: Set up Ollama with Qwen2.5-Coder 7B or Qwen3 8B for local LLM inference. Verify GPU acceleration (RTX 3060 Ti, 8GB VRAM) is working correctly. Test model performance on classification tasks to establish baseline latency and quality metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Ollama is running with Qwen3 8B or Qwen2.5-Coder 7B model
GPU acceleration is confirmed (check nvidia-smi during inference)
Model responds to test prompts with <200ms latency
Model can classify sample lead data (industry, size, intent) with >80% accuracy

stories.title: Build Local Ollama Router N8N Node
stories.description: Create an N8N custom node or HTTP request workflow that calls Ollama API for local LLM tasks. Implement handlers for: lead classification (industry, company size, intent signals), lead scoring (engagement metrics, ICP fit), simple email subject line generation, and contact deduplication matching. Include proper error handling and timeout logic.
acceptanceCriteria: N8N node/workflow can call Ollama API at localhost:11434
Lead classification returns structured JSON with industry, size, intent
Lead scoring returns numeric score (0-100) with confidence level
Subject line generation returns 3-5 variants per request
Contact deduplication returns match probability and conflicting fields
All Ollama calls complete in <500ms for simple tasks
Errors are logged and return graceful fallback responses

stories.title: Build Claude API Router N8N Node
stories.description: Create an N8N custom node or HTTP request workflow that calls Claude API for complex LLM tasks. Implement handlers for: personalized email body generation (using lead context and ICP), complex lead qualification reasoning, campaign strategy recommendations, and multi-step workflow design. Include token counting and cost tracking for every API call.
acceptanceCriteria: N8N node/workflow can call Claude API with proper authentication
Email body generation produces personalized 150-300 word emails
Lead qualification returns reasoning chain and confidence score
Campaign strategy returns multi-step recommendations with rationale
All Claude API calls log: timestamp, tokens used, cost, task type
Token counts are accurate (input + output tokens)
API errors trigger retry logic (max 3 attempts with exponential backoff)

stories.title: Implement Task Routing Classifier Logic
stories.description: Build the routing decision layer that analyzes incoming tasks and decides whether to route to Ollama (local) or Claude API (cloud). Classification criteria: simple/fast tasks (classification, scoring, deduplication) → Ollama; complex/creative tasks (personalized content, reasoning, strategy) → Claude. Implement fallback mechanism: if Ollama fails or returns low-confidence result, automatically retry with Claude API.
acceptanceCriteria: Router correctly classifies 10 sample tasks (5 simple, 5 complex)
Simple tasks route to Ollama with >90% accuracy
Complex tasks route to Claude API with >90% accuracy
Ollama failures trigger automatic Claude API fallback within 1 second
Low-confidence Ollama responses (<0.7) trigger Claude fallback
Routing decisions are logged with: task type, chosen LLM, confidence, latency
Router handles edge cases (empty input, malformed requests) gracefully

stories.title: Implement Cost Tracking and Budget Alerting System
stories.description: Build a cost tracking system that logs all Claude API calls with token counts and calculates running costs. Track cost per lead processed and aggregate daily spend. Implement alerting mechanism that triggers when daily spend exceeds configurable threshold (default: $10/day). Store cost data in N8N database or external log for reporting.
acceptanceCriteria: All Claude API calls are logged with: timestamp, task_id, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost
Cost calculation uses current Claude API pricing (e.g., $3/MTok input, $15/MTok output)
Daily spend is aggregated and stored (reset at midnight UTC)
Alert triggers when daily spend exceeds threshold (configurable)
Cost per lead is calculated (total spend / leads processed)
Cost dashboard or report is accessible via N8N or external log
Cost data persists across N8N restarts

stories.title: Create End-to-End Verification Tests for Dual-Core LLM Routing
stories.description: Build comprehensive verification tests that validate the entire routing system. Test routing logic with sample leads, verify local LLM handles classification correctly, verify Claude generates quality personalized content, and confirm fallback mechanism works under failure scenarios (Ollama down, low confidence, timeout).
acceptanceCriteria: Test suite includes 20+ sample leads with varying complexity
Routing logic achieves >90% accuracy on test set
Local LLM classification tests pass with >80% accuracy
Claude personalized email tests achieve >4/5 quality score (human review)
Fallback mechanism tests pass: Ollama failure → Claude retry within 1s
Low-confidence fallback tests pass: <0.7 score → Claude retry
Cost tracking tests verify accurate token counting and spend calculation
All tests run in N8N test workflows or external test harness

stories.title: Document Dual-Core LLM Routing System Architecture and Operations
stories.description: Create comprehensive documentation covering: system architecture (routing logic flow diagram), N8N workflow setup (Ollama and Claude nodes), configuration guide (API keys, model selection, cost thresholds), operational runbook (monitoring, troubleshooting, fallback scenarios), and cost optimization strategies. Include decision tree for when to use Ollama vs Claude.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture document includes routing logic flow diagram
N8N workflow setup guide with screenshots and JSON exports
Configuration guide covers: Ollama setup, Claude API keys, cost thresholds
Operational runbook includes: monitoring dashboards, common errors, fallback procedures
Cost optimization guide documents: task complexity thresholds, batching strategies, model selection
Decision tree diagram shows when to use Ollama vs Claude for each task type
All documentation is stored in N8N project documentation or wiki

stories.title: Prepare Lead Import Data Source and Validate Format
stories.description: Identify and prepare the ~30,000 lead CSV/database export for import into Mautic. Validate data format, field completeness, and deduplicate at the source. Create field mapping specification for Mautic import (name, email, company, title, industry, state, source attribution). Document any data quality issues and remediation steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Lead data source location documented (CSV file path or database connection)
CSV/database export contains ~30,000 leads with required fields
Field mapping specification created: CSV columns → Mautic custom fields
Data validation performed: email format, required fields, null checks
Source-level deduplication completed (remove exact duplicates)
Data quality report generated: completeness rates per field, invalid emails
Source attribution field preserved (original lead source/campaign)
Remediation plan documented for data quality issues

stories.title: Import Lead Database into Mautic via API or CLI
stories.description: Execute bulk import of ~30,000 leads into Mautic using Mautic API or CLI import tool. Implement batching strategy (500-1000 leads per batch) to avoid timeouts. Map CSV fields to Mautic custom fields. Enable deduplication against existing Mautic contacts (match on email). Log import progress and capture any import errors for review.
acceptanceCriteria: Import script uses Mautic API or CLI for bulk import
Batching strategy implemented (500-1000 leads per batch)
CSV field mapping applied: name, email, company, title, industry, state, source
Email-based deduplication enabled (skip or update existing contacts)
Import progress logged with batch number and success/failure counts
Import errors captured to error log with lead data for retry
Test import with 100 sample leads before full import
All tests pass with mock CSV data and Mautic API responses

stories.title: Create Mautic Segments for Industry and State
stories.description: Build Mautic segments for targeted campaigns based on lead attributes. Create segments by industry (Higher Education, Healthcare, Manufacturing), state (AR, UT, NM), and organization size. Configure segment filters using custom field values from imported leads. Verify segment populations after import completes.
acceptanceCriteria: Segment created: Industry = Higher Education with appropriate filter
Segment created: Industry = Healthcare with appropriate filter
Segment created: Industry = Manufacturing with appropriate filter
Segment created: State = AR (Arkansas) with appropriate filter
Segment created: State = UT (Utah) with appropriate filter
Segment created: State = NM (New Mexico) with appropriate filter
Segment created: Organization Size (Small <50, Medium 50-500, Large >500)
Segment filters use custom field values from imported lead data
Test script verifies segments populate correctly with sample data

stories.title: Create VMware Users Segment Based on Available Data
stories.description: Analyze imported lead data to identify VMware users based on available fields (company name, industry, keywords in notes/tags). Create Mautic segment filter logic to capture VMware-related leads. Document assumptions and criteria used for VMware user identification. Verify segment population and spot-check accuracy.
acceptanceCriteria: VMware user identification criteria documented (e.g., company name contains 'VMware', tags, notes)
Mautic segment created with multi-field filter logic for VMware users
Segment filter checks: company field, tags, notes, custom VMware indicator field
Spot-check 10 random leads in segment to verify VMware association
Segment population documented (expected vs actual count)
Test script verifies segment logic with mock VMware-related lead data
False positive rate assessed and documented

stories.title: Configure Mautic Lead Scoring Rules
stories.description: Implement Mautic lead scoring rules with demographic and engagement components. Demographic score based on title (C-level, VP, Director, Manager), industry (target industries get higher scores), and company size. Engagement score based on email opens, clicks, page visits. Negative scoring for bounces, unsubscribes, inactivity. Define MQL threshold (e.g., 50 points).
acceptanceCriteria: Demographic scoring rules configured: C-level (+20), VP (+15), Director (+10), Manager (+5)
Industry scoring rules configured: Higher Ed/Healthcare/Manufacturing (+10)
Company size scoring rules configured: >500 employees (+15), 50-500 (+10), <50 (+5)
Engagement scoring rules configured: Email open (+5), Click (+10), Page visit (+15)
Negative scoring rules configured: Bounce (-10), Unsubscribe (-20), 90-day inactivity (-5)
MQL threshold defined at 50 points and documented
Scoring rules automatically apply to imported leads on import
Test lead progression verified: score updates on activities

stories.title: Verify Import Results and Segment Population
stories.description: Build verification script to validate import accuracy, segment populations, and lead scoring. Verify total import count matches expected ~30,000 leads. Spot-check 10 random contacts for data accuracy. Verify all segments populate with expected counts. Confirm lead scoring is calculating correctly on imported leads. Generate import summary report.
acceptanceCriteria: Verification script queries Mautic API for total contact count
Total contact count matches expected ~30,000 (within 1% tolerance)
Script spot-checks 10 random contacts and verifies field accuracy
Script verifies each segment population > 0 and matches expected distribution
Script verifies lead scoring is active (scores > 0 for qualified leads)
Import summary report generated with counts, segment populations, data quality metrics
Test script runs against Mautic API and outputs clear pass/fail status
All verification tests pass after full import

stories.title: Document Lead Import and Segmentation Procedures
stories.description: Create comprehensive documentation for lead import process, segment configuration, and lead scoring setup. Include step-by-step import procedure, field mappings, segment filter logic, scoring rules, and ongoing maintenance tasks (periodic deduplication, segment review, scoring rule adjustments). Document troubleshooting steps and common issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Lead import procedure documented: data prep, field mapping, batching, error handling
Field mapping table documented: CSV columns → Mautic custom fields
Segment configuration documented: filters, logic, expected populations
Lead scoring rules documented: demographic, engagement, negative scoring, MQL threshold
Ongoing maintenance tasks documented: deduplication, segment review, scoring adjustments
